core_model.structure_summary: A collection of scalar values indexed by tuples over a finite alphabet is re-expressed through fixed orthogonal sign-weighted linear combinations, and minimal linear dependence relations among these combinations are characterized.
core_model.canonical_form: W_S = \sum_{v} \chi_S(v)\, w_v,\quad \chi_S(v)\in\{+1,-1\};\qquad w_{11} = w_{10} + w_{01} - w_{00}\ \text{(zero-interaction / circuit relations)}

variables.meaning: scalar value assigned to each tuple over a finite index alphabet
fixed sign pattern defining each orthogonal transform coefficient

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A fixed linear (orthogonal sign-weighted) transform of a finite array of scalars together with the minimal linear dependence relations (vanishing higher-order combinations) among the transformed quantities.

notes: No dynamics: the paper's core object is a static linear-algebraic structure (a sign-weighted orthogonal transform, i.e. Walsh/Hadamard-type coefficients, plus minimal linear dependence relations 'circuits') on a finite grid of scalars indexed by tuples over an alphabet of size >=2. Structural-feature vocabulary is a poor fit for pure algebra; I used conservation_law for the vanishing linear-combination relations and pairwise_coupling for the two-index interaction terms. Lower confidence reflects the vocabulary mismatch rather than uncertainty about the object.
